full_text:
Severe heatwave and unseasonal rains batter AP, Telangana
The Telugu-speaking states of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana are grappling with extreme weather conditions as intense heatwaves coincide with unexpected rainfall and thunderstorms.
The Telugu-speaking states of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana are grappling with extreme weather conditions as intense heatwaves coincide with unexpected rainfall and thunderstorms. With temperatures soaring to record levels at the very start of May, authorities have issued urgent advisories urging residents—particularly farmers—to remain cautious.
In Andhra Pradesh, Prakasam district recorded the highest temperature in the state, with Kopperapadu registering a scorching 45.4°C on Saturday. Several other districts, including Tirupati, Nellore and Palnadu, reported temperatures ranging between 44°C and 45°C. Notably, over 239 mandals experienced temperatures exceeding 41°C, highlighting the widespread severity of the heat. Officials have warned that as many as 124 mandals are likely to face severe heatwave conditions on Sunday.
Telangana is witnessing equally intense conditions. Nizamabad district recorded a peak temperature of 46°C, the highest so far this season. Other districts such as Nalgonda, Nirmal and Siddipet are also experiencing extreme heat. In the capital, Hyderabad, temperatures reached 42.6°C in areas including Mushirabad. According to the Meteorological Centre, the heatwave is expected to persist for the next two days, with a slight drop in temperatures anticipated thereafter.
Compounding the situation, a surface trough is triggering unseasonal rains in parts of the region. Light showers accompanied by lightning are likely in North Andhra districts, including Srikakulam, Vizianagaram and Visakhapatnam. Gusty winds are also expected in parts of Telangana. Officials have cautioned that rising ground temperatures can lead to sudden cloud formation, increasing the risk of lightning strikes. People are advised not to take shelter under trees or near electric poles during such conditions.
The untimely rains pose a serious threat to farmers, particularly as crops approach harvest. Authorities have urged farmers to secure harvested produce by moving it to safe locations or covering it with tarpaulins.
For the general public, officials recommend avoiding outdoor activities between 11:00 am and 4:00 pm. If going out is unavoidable, residents are advised to use umbrellas or cover their heads, and to stay well-hydrated by consuming sufficient water or oral rehydration solutions.
Authorities continue to monitor the evolving situation closely as the region faces the dual challenge of extreme heat and unpredictable weather.

title: Chicken Prices Surge In Telangana Amid Heatwave Impact - INDToday
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
Hyderabad, May 3: Chicken prices surge in Telangana as extreme summer heat disrupts poultry supply across the region. The sharp rise has added pressure on consumers already dealing with high temperatures.
According to market sources, soaring temperatures have severely affected poultry farms. As a result, large numbers of birds have died due to heat stress. Therefore, supply to markets has dropped significantly in recent days.
Although demand usually dips during summer, the sudden fall in supply has pushed prices upward. Within a week, chicken prices increased by nearly ₹40 per kg. Consequently, consumers now face higher costs for a staple protein source.
At present, prices in districts like Warangal and Nizamabad have reached around ₹270 per kg. Meanwhile, in Hyderabad, rates have climbed to nearly ₹280 per kg.
The situation appears more severe in Andhra Pradesh. In areas such as Srikakulam and Pithapuram, chicken prices have touched ₹300 per kg. Hence, consumers across both Telugu states are feeling the impact.
Experts say the poultry sector faces seasonal challenges every year. However, this year’s intense heat has worsened the situation. Moreover, fluctuating weather conditions have increased mortality rates among birds.
Traders expect prices to remain high in the short term. Only after temperatures drop and new batches of poultry enter the market will supply stabilize. Until then, chicken lovers may continue to face elevated prices.

full_text:
Skip to contentNizamabad Mendora records 46°C amid heatwave
HYDERABAD: Severe heatwave conditions continue across Telangana, with daytime temperatures resembling furnace-like conditions. Mendora in Nizamabad district recorded a peak of 46°C, the highest in recent years.
Several districts reported temperatures above 45°C. Nirmal and Siddipet recorded 45.9°C, while Kamanpur in Peddapalli district touched 45.8°C. Multiple locations across Jagtial, Asifabad, Suryapet and neighbouring districts recorded temperatures between 45.1°C and 45.7°C.
Hyderabad also witnessed intense heat, with Amberpet and Musheerabad recording a maximum of 42.6°C. Residents struggled under the harsh conditions.
Heatstroke deaths reported in several districts
At least four people died due to heatstroke in different parts of the state. Tejavathu Chapli, 75, from Ammapalem village in Khammam district, died early Saturday after suffering heatstroke two days earlier.
Vaddepalli Shankar, 65, a retired employee from Kothagudem, died after falling ill due to extreme heat. Venkanna, 53, from Kuravi in Mahabubabad district, collapsed while grazing cattle and was declared dead at hospital.
Jagati Radha, 52, from Maredupalli in Jagtial district, suffered heatstroke while attending her son’s wedding preparations. She died while being shifted to a hospital in Karimnagar.
Lightning strikes claim two lives amid sudden storm
Amid the heatwave, strong winds and rain caused damage in Kothagudem district, soaking paddy and maize crops in several mandals.
Kondru Vinod, 27, from Ganugapadu in Bhadradri district, died after lightning struck him while he was in a maize field with friends. Three others escaped.
In a separate incident, farmer Gopayya, 50, from Iravendi in Burgampadu mandal, died after being struck by lightning while working in his field.
No immediate relief; rain forecast in some districts
The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre said maximum temperatures are unlikely to change over the next two days. A drop of 2–4°C is expected after four days.
Light to moderate rainfall is likely over the next three days in districts including Asifabad, Mancherial, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Khammam, Nalgonda, Hanumakonda, Jangaon, Siddipet, Bhongir and Nagarkurnool.
